senses.definition: Toward or situated near the front end or beak (rostrum) of an animal, especially the nose or snout area.
examples: The rostral part of the skull is near the beak in this species.
The rostral bones extend toward the front of the head.

senses.definition: Toward the rostral or front part of the body; toward the head end.
examples: The nerve fibers project rostrally from the brainstem.
The beak is rostrally oriented in this specimen.

senses.definition: To provide with a beak-like projection or rostrum; to form a beak-shaped extension on; to have a rostrum.
examples: The crustacean rostrates its carapace to defend itself.
Some fossil species rostrate their snouts for feeding.

senses.definition: Having a rostrum or beak-like projection.
examples: A rostrated fish displays a pointed anterior snout.
The fossil shows rostrated features on the skull.

senses.definition: Bearing or producing a rostrum or beak-like projection.
examples: The rostriferous limb features a sharp anterior projection.
Some extinct species are rostriferous, with prominent snouts.

senses.definition: Having the shape or form of a rostrum; beak-shaped.
examples: The rostriform shell narrows toward the front.
A rostriform crest marks the skull.

senses.definition: Relating to the rostrum (beak-like projection) and the antennae, typically describing anatomical arrangement.
examples: Rostroantennary nerves were mapped in the specimen.
The rostroantennary region shows paired projections.

senses.definition: Relating to the rostrum and the gill region or branchial structures.
examples: Rostrobranchial muscles were identified in the specimen.
The rostrоbranchial axis is used in classification.

senses.definition: Having a beak-like keel or carinate rostrum.
examples: A rostrocarinate projection enhances piercing capability.
The fossil shows rostrocarinate features.

senses.definition: Species or structures that have a rostrocarinate feature; organisms with rostral keels.
examples: The rostrocarinates display pronounced rostral keels.
Paleontologists describe several rostrocarinates in the assemblage.

senses.definition: Relating to both the rostral (front) and caudal (back) ends; the front-to-back axis.
examples: The rostrocaudal axis is a key concept in anatomy.
Rostrocaudal progression indicates movement from head to tail.

senses.definition: A shape or form resembling a beak or rostrum; a rostral-like projection.
examples: The rostroid projection helps identify the species.
Researchers noted a rostroid structure on the fossil skull.

senses.definition: Located at or toward the rostral and lateral sides; front-side placement.
examples: The rostrolateral margin marks the boundary.
Rostrolateral nerves emerge near the front sides.

senses.definition: Relating to the rostrum; beak-like in form.
examples: The rostrular projection aids in respiration.
A rostrular crest spans the anterior face.

senses.definition: To form a beak-like projection; to shape like a rostrum.
examples: The coral rostrulate along the rim as it grows.
Certain taxa rostrulate their anterior structures.

senses.definition: A small beak-like projection or rostrum; a rostral structure.
examples: The fossil displays a distinct rostulum at the anterior edge.
Researchers measured the rostulum length.

senses.definition: A beak-like projection on the face of an animal, or the elongated anterior part of a skull or exoskeleton resembling a beak.
examples: The rostrum of the dolphin is elongated and streamlined.
In this fossil, the rostrum is particularly prominent.

senses.definition: Plural of rostrum; multiple beak-like projections or beaks.
examples: The fossil showed several rostrums along the head region.
Different species have rostrums of varying lengths.

senses.definition: A plural or plural-like form of rostrum; or a short form for rosters in certain contexts, though in scientific terms this is uncommon.
examples: The specimen catalog lists several rosts in the cranial region.
In some texts, rosts is used as a variant plural of rostrum.

senses.definition: A composite or mis-spelled term; no established sense in standard dictionaries.
examples: No standard definition; could be a typographical variant.

senses.definition: A small rosette or rostral-like feature; used in pathology to describe a small cluster or arrangement resembling a rosette.
examples: The biopsy showed a rosula formation in the tissue.
Rosula pattern can appear in certain tumors.

senses.definition: Resembling or arranged as a rosula; rosette-like.
examples: The rosular arrangement appears in the specimen.
Rosular patterns are observed in the cross-section.

senses.definition: Plural of rosula; multiple rosule or rosette-like structures.
examples: Several rosulas were found in the sample.
The rosulas correspond to vascular rosettes.

senses.definition: To form a rosula or rosette-like cluster; to create rosette structures.
examples: The tissue roslate to form a rosulate core.
Certain cells rosulate under specific conditions.

senses.definition: Having a pinkish or reddish color; cheerful or hopeful.
examples: Her cheeks were rosy after running.
The forecast gave rosy prospects for the market.
